description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple large photovoltaic panels mounted on a rectangular base. The panels are arranged in two main clusters, each set at a noticeable tilt, likely to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be a flat platform, possibly simulating a rooftop or ground installation, with a small central structure that could house electrical components. The panels are supported by visible frames, likely made of metal, and the surface of the panels is dark and grid-patterned, characteristic of solar cells. There are no visible cables or wiring on the exterior, and the installation is bordered by a thin red edge on one side. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted or flat-roof solar array suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A large, fixed solar panel array with two tilted clusters on a rectangular base, featuring a central housing structure and metallic support frames.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main clusters of tilted solar panels on a flat base, with a small central housing structure visible between them.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the base, with the panel arrangement and support frames more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the solar panels and the full width of the base, with the central housing structure and support frames visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tilt and spacing of the solar panels, as well as the small central structure and a vertical post on the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, facing toward the front (likely south), fixed orientation
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted or flat-roof mounted with visible metallic (aluminum or steel) support frames
attribute_array_configuration: two main clusters of panels, each in a multi-row arrangement, approximately 8-10 panels total, spaced apart on a rectangular base
